rationale_for_baseline_tier: Minor recurrent aphthous stomatitis is self-limiting and managed at home with topical therapy (T1). The modifier here is dehydration risk from pain-driven poor intake in a patient living alone, which is addressed by analgesia-enabled hydration plus a clear dehydration safety-net to ED (T4); if she cannot rehydrate, escalate. Persistent single ulcer >3 weeks carries a GP/biopsy pathway.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to maintain fluids despite topical anaesthetic, not passing urine, or marked lightheadedness/weakness
escalation_edges.rationale: Failed oral rehydration / dehydration may need IV fluids, especially living alone.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you still can't drink even with the numbing gel and straw, stop passing urine, or feel very lightheaded or weak — go to the Emergency Department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single ulcer that does not heal within 3 weeks, or fever with spreading lesions / swollen glands
escalation_edges.rationale: Persistent ulcer needs biopsy to exclude malignancy; systemic features need assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; no oral intake today
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for ED if rehydration fails; arrange a definite check-in and give explicit dehydration red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use the numbing gel and steroid paste, and drink through a straw past your front teeth so you keep your fluids up. The ulcers should ease over the next few days and heal within a couple of weeks.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP review in 7–10 days; ED if you can't rehydrate
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED if you cannot drink at all or feel very unwell overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Minor recurrent aphthous stomatitis; HSV/malignancy screen negative; at dehydration risk (lives alone, poor intake); topical steroid + analgesia; consider haematinics.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Pure self-care with no dehydration safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A patient living alone cannot drink, becomes progressively dehydrated overnight, and presents late.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not addressing hydration or omitting the >3-week persistent-ulcer safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for typical minor aphthae that rehydrate with topical anaesthesia — unnecessary.
